import psycopg2
import os
from dotenv import load_dotenv
load_dotenv()

try:
    conn = psycopg2.connect(os.getenv(''DATABASE_URL''))
    cur = conn.cursor()
    
    # Check if suppliers table exists
    cur.execute(""SELECT table_name FROM information_schema.tables WHERE table_name = ''suppliers''"")
    table_exists = cur.fetchone()
    print(f''Suppliers table exists: {table_exists is not None}'')
    
    if table_exists:
        # Check suppliers count
        cur.execute(''SELECT COUNT(*) FROM suppliers'')
        count = cur.fetchone()[0]
        print(f''Number of suppliers: {count}'')
        
        # Get sample data
        cur.execute(''SELECT id, company_name, contact_person, email FROM suppliers LIMIT 5'')
        rows = cur.fetchall()
        print(''Sample suppliers:'')
        for row in rows:
            print(f''  ID: {row[0]}, Company: {row[1]}, Contact: {row[2]}, Email: {row[3]}'')
    
    cur.close()
    conn.close()
except Exception as e:
    print(f''Error: {e}'')
")

Bash(/c/Users/foodz/AppData/Local/Programs/Python/Python312/python.exe -c "
import sqlite3
import os

try:
    # Connect to SQLite database
    db_path = ''./foodxchange.db''
    if os.path.exists(db_path):
        conn = sqlite3.connect(db_path)
        cur = conn.cursor()
        
        # Check if suppliers table exists
        cur.execute(""SELECT name FROM sqlite_master WHERE type=''table'' AND name=''suppliers''"")
        table_exists = cur.fetchone()
        print(f''Suppliers table exists: {table_exists is not None}'')
        
        if table_exists:
            # Check suppliers count
            cur.execute(''SELECT COUNT(*) FROM suppliers'')
            count = cur.fetchone()[0]
            print(f''Number of suppliers: {count}'')
            
            # Get sample data
            cur.execute(''SELECT id, company_name, contact_person, email FROM suppliers LIMIT 5'')
            rows = cur.fetchall()
            print(''Sample suppliers:'')
            for row in rows:
                print(f''  ID: {row[0]}, Company: {row[1]}, Contact: {row[2]}, Email: {row[3]}'')
        else:
            print(''Suppliers table does not exist'')
        
        # List all tables
        cur.execute(""SELECT name FROM sqlite_master WHERE type=''table''"")
        tables = cur.fetchall()
        print(f''Available tables: {[t[0] for t in tables]}'')
        
        cur.close()
        conn.close()
    else:
        print(f''Database file does not exist at: {db_path}'')
except Exception as e:
    print(f''Error: {e}'')
")

import psycopg2
import os
from dotenv import load_dotenv
load_dotenv()

try:
    conn = psycopg2.connect(os.getenv(''DATABASE_URL''))
    cur = conn.cursor()
    
    # Check if suppliers table exists
    cur.execute(""SELECT table_name FROM information_schema.tables WHERE table_name = ''suppliers''"")
    table_exists = cur.fetchone()
    print(f''Suppliers table exists: {table_exists is not None}'')
    
    if table_exists:
        # Check suppliers count
        cur.execute(''SELECT COUNT(*) FROM suppliers'')
        count = cur.fetchone()[0]
        print(f''Number of suppliers: {count}'')
        
        # Get sample data if any exists
        if count > 0:
            cur.execute(''SELECT id, company_name, contact_person, email FROM suppliers LIMIT 5'')
            rows = cur.fetchall()
            print(''Sample suppliers:'')
            for row in rows:
                print(f''  ID: {row[0]}, Company: {row[1]}, Contact: {row[2]}, Email: {row[3]}'')
        else:

    cur.close()
    conn.close()

Bash(/c/Users/foodz/AppData/Local/Programs/Python/Python312/python.exe -c "
import psycopg2
import os
from dotenv import load_dotenv
load_dotenv()

try:
    conn = psycopg2.connect(os.getenv(''DATABASE_URL''))
    cur = conn.cursor()
    
    # Check if there are any tables with supplier data
    cur.execute(""SELECT table_name FROM information_schema.tables WHERE table_schema = ''public'' AND table_name LIKE ''%supplier%''"")
    supplier_tables = cur.fetchall()
    print(''Tables with supplier in name:'')
    for table in supplier_tables:
        print(f''  {table[0]}'')
        
    # Check for data in other related tables
    tables_to_check = [''users'', ''contractors'', ''buyers_suppliers'']
    for table in tables_to_check:
        try:
            cur.execute(f""SELECT table_name FROM information_schema.tables WHERE table_name = ''{table}''"")
            if cur.fetchone():
                cur.execute(f''SELECT COUNT(*) FROM {table}'')
                count = cur.fetchone()[0]
                print(f''{table}: {count} records'')
                
                if count > 0 and table == ''contractors'':
                    cur.execute(''SELECT id, name, email, role FROM contractors LIMIT 3'')
                    rows = cur.fetchall()
                    print(''  Sample contractors:'')
                    for row in rows:
                        print(f''    ID: {row[0]}, Name: {row[1]}, Email: {row[2]}, Role: {row[3]}'')
        except Exception as e:
            print(f''Error checking {table}: {e}'')
    
    cur.close()
    conn.close()
except Exception as e:
    print(f''Error: {e}'')
")

import psycopg2
import os
from dotenv import load_dotenv
from datetime import datetime

load_dotenv()

try:
    conn = psycopg2.connect(os.getenv(''DATABASE_URL''))
    cur = conn.cursor()
    
    # Insert sample suppliers
    sample_suppliers = [
        (''Global Food Solutions Ltd'', ''Maria Rodriguez'', ''maria@globalfood.com'', ''+1-555-0123'', ''123 Trade Street, New York, NY'', ''USA'', ''ISO 22000, HACCP'', True),
        (''Mediterranean Imports Co'', ''Giuseppe Rossi'', ''giuseppe@medimports.com'', ''+39-06-555-0234'', ''Via Roma 45, Rome'', ''Italy'', ''BRC, IFS'', True),
        (''Asian Spice Trading'', ''Raj Patel'', ''raj@asianspice.com'', ''+91-22-555-0345'', ''Mumbai Trade Center, Mumbai'', ''India'', ''FSSAI, Organic'', False),
        (''Nordic Seafood AS'', ''Erik Hansen'', ''erik@nordicsea.no'', ''+47-555-0456'', ''Fisherman\''s Wharf, Bergen'', ''Norway'', ''MSC, ASC'', True),
        (''Brazilian Coffee Exporters'', ''Carlos Silva'', ''carlos@brcoffee.com'', ''+55-11-555-0567'', ''Santos Port, São Paulo'', ''Brazil'', ''Fair Trade, Rainforest Alliance'', False)
    ]
    
    for supplier in sample_suppliers:
        cur.execute(''''''
            INSERT INTO suppliers (company_name, contact_person, email, phone, address, country, certifications, verified, created_at, updated_at)
            VALUES (%s, %s, %s, %s, %s, %s, %s, %s, %s, %s)
        '''''', supplier + (datetime.now(), datetime.now()))
    
    conn.commit()
    
    # Verify the data was inserted
    cur.execute(''SELECT COUNT(*) FROM suppliers'')
    count = cur.fetchone()[0]
    print(f''Successfully added sample data. Total suppliers: {count}'')
    
    # Show sample data
    cur.execute(''SELECT company_name, contact_person, country, verified FROM suppliers'')
    suppliers = cur.fetchall()
    print(''Suppliers added:'')
    for supplier in suppliers:
        status = ''✓ Verified'' if supplier[3] else ''⏳ Pending''
        print(f''  • {supplier[0]} ({supplier[2]}) - {supplier[1]} - {status}'')
    
    cur.close()
    conn.close()
    
except Exception as e:
    print(f''Error: {e}'')
")

import psycopg2
import os
from dotenv import load_dotenv

load_dotenv()

try:
    conn = psycopg2.connect(os.getenv(''DATABASE_URL''))
    cur = conn.cursor()
    
    # Test the same query that the suppliers page uses
    cur.execute(''''''
        SELECT id, company_name, contact_person, email, phone, 
               address, country, verified, created_at
        FROM suppliers 
        ORDER BY created_at DESC
        LIMIT 100
    '''''')
    
    rows = cur.fetchall()
    print(f''Query returned {len(rows)} suppliers:'')
    
    for row in rows:
        status = ''Verified'' if row[7] else ''Pending''
        print(f''  ID: {row[0]}, Company: {row[1]}, Contact: {row[2]}, Country: {row[6]}, Status: {status}'')
    
    cur.close()
    conn.close()
    
except Exception as e:
    print(f''Error: {e}'')
")

import psycopg2
import os
from dotenv import load_dotenv
load_dotenv()

try:
    conn = psycopg2.connect(os.getenv(''DATABASE_URL''))
    cur = conn.cursor()
    
    # Clear projects table
    cur.execute(''DELETE FROM projects'')
    deleted_count = cur.rowcount
    
    # Also drop the table completely to start fresh
    cur.execute(''DROP TABLE IF EXISTS projects CASCADE'')
    
    conn.commit()
    cur.close()
    conn.close()
    
    print(f''✅ Deleted {deleted_count} project records'')
    print(''✅ Dropped projects table completely'')
    
except Exception as e:
    print(f''❌ Database error: {e}'')
")

Bash(/c/Users/foodz/AppData/Local/Programs/Python/Python312/python.exe -c "
import psycopg2
import os
from dotenv import load_dotenv
load_dotenv()

conn = psycopg2.connect(os.getenv(''DATABASE_URL''))
cur = conn.cursor()

# Check suppliers table
cur.execute(''SELECT COUNT(*) FROM suppliers'')
count = cur.fetchone()[0]
print(f''Total suppliers: {count:,}'')

# Get sample data
cur.execute(''''''
    SELECT supplier_name, company_name, company_email, country, supplier_type 
    FROM suppliers 
    LIMIT 5
'''''')
rows = cur.fetchall()
print(''\nSample suppliers:'')
for row in rows:
    print(f''  {row[0]} | {row[1]} | {row[2]} | {row[3]} | {row[4][:50]}...'' if row[4] and len(row[4]) > 50 else f''  {row[0]} | {row[1]} | {row[2]} | {row[3]} | {row[4]}'')

# Get country distribution
cur.execute(''''''
    SELECT country, COUNT(*) as cnt 
    FROM suppliers 
    WHERE country IS NOT NULL 
    GROUP BY country 
    ORDER BY cnt DESC 
    LIMIT 10
'''''')
countries = cur.fetchall()
print(''\nTop 10 countries:'')
for country, cnt in countries:
    print(f''  {country}: {cnt:,}'')

cur.close()
conn.close()
")

Bash(/c/Users/foodz/AppData/Local/Programs/Python/Python312/python.exe -c "
import psycopg2
import os
from dotenv import load_dotenv
load_dotenv()

conn = psycopg2.connect(os.getenv(''DATABASE_URL''))
cur = conn.cursor()

# Check progress
cur.execute(''''''
    SELECT 
        COUNT(*) as total,
        COUNT(CASE WHEN products IS NOT NULL AND LENGTH(products) > 10 THEN 1 END) as enhanced,
        COUNT(CASE WHEN products IS NULL OR LENGTH(products) < 10 THEN 1 END) as need_enhancement
    FROM suppliers
'''''')
stats = cur.fetchone()

print(f''Enhancement Progress:'')
print(f''Total suppliers: {stats[0]:,}'')
print(f''Already enhanced: {stats[1]:,} ({stats[1]*100//stats[0]}%)'')
print(f''Still need enhancement: {stats[2]:,} ({stats[2]*100//stats[0]}%)'')

# Sample enhanced suppliers
cur.execute(''''''
    SELECT supplier_name, products 
    FROM suppliers 
    WHERE products IS NOT NULL AND LENGTH(products) > 50
    ORDER BY id DESC
    LIMIT 3
'''''')

print(''\nRecently enhanced:'')
for row in cur.fetchall():
    print(f''- {row[0]}'')
    print(f''  Products: {row[1][:80]}...'')

cur.close()
conn.close()
")
